name: Thai Chuu-Chii (Red Curry) of Coffin Bay Scallops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mortar and pestle or spice grinder, grind white peppercorns and mace to a powder.
2. Roast Thai shrimp paste in a microwave oven or under the grill until fragrant.
3. Combine pepper-mace mixture, roasted shrimp paste, long red dried chillies (deseeded and chopped), shallots (or Spanish onion), lemon grass stalk, galangal, kaffir lime zest, coriander root, salt, and water in a blender or food processor. Process until a very fine paste is formed.
4. Transfer the paste to a storage jar.
5. In a medium-sized pan, heat coconut cream over high heat until oil separates out (about 3-5 minutes). If using canned coconut cream, use the solid mass at the top of the can and add enough liquid to make up 250 ml.
6. Add red curry paste to the heated coconut cream and fry for 5 minutes, stirring, until fragrant.
7. Stir in fish sauce and palm sugar, then cook until the color deepens.
8. Add Coffin Bay scallops, coconut milk, kaffir lime leaves, fresh red chilli, and coriander or basil leaves. Adjust sweet-sour balance as needed.
9. Serve with jasmine rice.
